mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative or flat performance when both GDPC1 and HOUST are rising, or pairwise correlation between proxies drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A 10-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening regime, causing bond yields to rise and equity valuations to contract, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or bond yields decline despite rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals improved manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through stronger corporate earnings.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the series exhibits mean reversion within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial jobless claims (PAYEMS) below 120k combined with rising wage growth (CPIAUCSL) indicates tight labor markets and inflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive trends when PAYEMS < 120k and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%, or the pair exhibits divergent movements.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% annualized decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when RSAFS declines >2% and HOUST falls >5%, or the proxies show divergent trends.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2% triggers inflationary expectations, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or rate hikes fail to materialize despite inflation spikes.
